note: Marlins in playoff position at the break, Dolphins camp opens this week, and a live chance the Fed hikes July 29.

items.title: Marlins enter the second half in playoff position - what will they do at the deadline?
items.blurb: At 52-45 and holding the third NL Wild Card, the Fish are surprise buyers ahead of the Aug 3 deadline. With Snelling done for the year, they are hunting arms without mortgaging the farm.

items.title: A July rate hike from the Fed? The odds are rising
items.blurb: FedWatch now puts roughly a 46 percent chance on a quarter-point hike at the July 28-29 meeting under a hawkish Warsh Fed. A hike would ripple straight through CRE debt pricing.

items.title: Dolphins announce 2026 training camp schedule
items.blurb: Rookies report July 21, vets July 28 for Jeff Hafley's first camp as head coach. First public practice is Aug 2 and all sessions are free to attend.

items.title: The Infatuation's July list of new Miami restaurant openings
items.blurb: Peche Mignon soft-opens in Edgewater as a champagne bar and cafe, Flight West brings seafood towers to South Miami, and Paris Baguette lands in Wynwood.

items.title: Panthers really happy with swift roster makeover
items.blurb: Brady Tkachuk, a new Markstrom-Schmid goalie tandem, and an eight-year Luostarinen deal in an 11-day blitz. The retool after missing the playoffs is basically done by mid-July.

items.title: Seaquarium site plans call for 2M SF on Virginia Key
items.blurb: Miami-Dade filed plans for the shuttered Seaquarium site with more than 2 million square feet and 877 boat slips. One of the biggest waterfront redevelopment plays in years.

items.title: FOMC minutes: inflation still well above 2 percent
items.blurb: June minutes show the committee held at 3.5 to 3.75 percent but flagged persistent inflation from oil and AI-driven price pressure. Sets the table for the late-July meeting.

items.title: CBRE: investors set to deploy more capital in 2026
items.blurb: Investment volume seen up 16 percent this year, with 55 percent of investors raising real estate allocations. Ample dry powder and tight spreads make a decent dealmaking backdrop.

items.title: Freedom Park leads $370M-plus week in construction permits
items.blurb: Inter Miami's Nu Stadium site moves ahead with 1M SF of retail and office, 750 hotel rooms and a 58-acre public park. The west side of the city keeps transforming.

items.title: Sunday Snippets: Stoic decatastrophizing
items.blurb: A tidy piece connecting ancient practice to modern CBT: worry starts with how you describe events, not the events themselves. Fitting for a Sunday reset.

items.title: What to watch on the Heat's Summer League team
items.blurb: The Vegas run wraps this weekend. Goldin and second-round pick Ryan Conwell have been the names to track, with Wayne Ellington coaching the group.

items.title: 9 most anticipated Miami restaurants opening summer 2026
items.blurb: The definitive watch list for the back half of summer if you are booking ahead.

items.title: Practical Stoicism: on procrastination and how to relax
items.blurb: Two July episodes worth a walk: what the Stoics actually said about putting things off, and whether relaxing is compatible with the discipline.
